tickerText: [TENNIS] Roland-Garros: Mirra Andreeva (19) wins her 1st Grand Slam title, defeating Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2! Men's Singles: Alexander Zverev def. Flavio Cobolli 6-1, 4-6, 6-4, 6-7(5), 6-1 to win his 1st Major! • [BADMINTON] Indonesia Open: Victor Lai (CAN) def. Jonatan Christie (INA) 21-19, 21-8; An Se-young (KOR) def. Akane Yamaguchi (JPN) 23-21, 21-12 • [SQUASH] British Open: 18-year-old Amina Orfi becomes the youngest women's champion in history (def. Nour El Sherbini 3-1) & Paul Coll clinches his 3rd title (def. Mostafa Asal 3-1) in Birmingham!

newsItems.title: Zverev and Andreeva Conquer Roland-Garros 2026
newsItems.excerpt: Alexander Zverev defeated Flavio Cobolli in a grueling 5-set final to win his first Grand Slam title, while 19-year-old Mirra Andreeva captured the women's singles title with a straight-sets victory.

newsItems.title: Victor Lai and An Se-young Triumph at Indonesia Open
newsItems.excerpt: Canada's Victor Lai stunned local favorite Jonatan Christie in straight games (21-19, 21-8), while top seed An Se-young overcame Akane Yamaguchi to defend her title.

newsItems.title: Coll Wins 3rd British Open; Orfi Makes Squash History
newsItems.excerpt: New Zealand's Paul Coll claimed his third British Open title after a 3-1 victory over Mostafa Asal, while 18-year-old Amina Orfi made history as the youngest women's champion ever.
